verification_notes: Extracted via pdftotext. Requires human verification against source PDF.

mysticism.description: Represents the depth of knowledge concerning a particular mystical path. Each incidence must be tied to a specific path (foundation of mystical knowledge). Paths are the mystical equivalent of Combat Styles: diverse talents and abilities folded into the umbrella skill.

meditation.description: The ability to reach a state of concentration by removing all extraneous distractions. Governs how many talents can be maintained simultaneously.
meditation.concentration_formula: Maximum cumulative Intensity of active talents = Meditation skill / 10
meditation.example: Meditation 66% = max Intensity 7, split across multiple talents (e.g., Augment X at 2, Invoke Y at 1, Enhance Z at 4)

aig_adaptation.note: AiG's Mysticism chapter was never published. Per Hannu (2026-05-16): 'Mysticism raw' — uses Mythras Core rules without adaptation. No Rune Affinity overlay specified for Mysticism.

paths.description: A path contains knowledge of multiple mental disciplines called talents: skill augmentations, trait invocations, and attribute enhancements. Paths are often based on ancient hermetic teachings, passed down in sacred books or by esoteric gurus or secret mountain-top schools.
paths.structure: Each path is tied to a profession or philosophy. Rare for an adept to learn more than one.

types.name: Augment
types.description: Skill augmentations — boost a specific skill by a number of grades equal to the Intensity applied

types.name: Invoke
types.description: Trait invocations — grant supernatural abilities or creature traits

types.name: Enhance
types.description: Attribute enhancements — boost attributes like Hit Points, Fatigue, Action Points

casting_mechanics.resource: No external resource consumed — talents are powered by concentration (Meditation skill)
casting_mechanics.activation: Requires a successful Mysticism skill roll to activate a talent
casting_mechanics.duration: Talents remain active as long as the mystic concentrates. Multiple talents can be maintained simultaneously up to the Meditation limit.
casting_mechanics.limitation: Effects do not extend beyond the mystic themselves — they become a tool to manipulate the world, but magic does not project outward

starting_at_chargen.note: A starting mystic chooses one path and learns talents from it. The number of starting talents is determined by cult/school membership (see Cults chapter p.196). Typically a mystic knows several talents from their path at creation.
starting_at_chargen.path_choice: One path chosen at character creation

glorantha_notes.applicability: Per AiG overview (p.60): 'The study of mysticism in Glorantha is limited to only those of Draconic blood or those deluded enough to become subservient to them.' Primarily Kralori and Draconic traditions.
glorantha_notes.current_cult_data: No cults in the current 94 one-pagers use Mysticism skills. Zero cults would use this system with current data.
glorantha_notes.future_use: Would become relevant if Kralori cultures or Draconic cults are added to the chargen.
